Terörist ilan edilmeyen parti, dernek kaldı mı ya? Yok; sendika kalmadı. Şimdi, bu totaliter rejimde denge-denetleme mekanizması diye bir şey kalmadı. Şimdi Parlamento var, bunu da işte bu yöntemle bastırmaya çalışıyorsunuz. Burada bile neredeyse bizim milletvekillerinin üzerinde bir vesayet kurulmaya çalışılıyor; bir baskı aracı. Niye korkalım sizden ya? En fazla arkadaşlarımız gibi bizi de hapse gönderirsiniz. Talimat verdiniz savcılara, hepimiz hakkında onlarca, yüzlerce fezleke hazırlandı. Yüksekdağ'a verilen cezanın yüzde 70'i yaptığı konuşmalar, düşünce ve ifade özgürlüğü. Yani en fazla bunu yapacaksınız, bunu da dünya görüyor yani yine hepimizin ayağına dolanıyor, en çok da sizin zalimliğiniz ifşa oluyor; bunu da görelim.

Şimdi, diğer yandan, yargıyı çok fazla tartışmayalım ama Kobani kumpas davasının bir turnusol kâğıdı olduğunu söylemeden geçmek istemiyorum. Talimat verdiğiniz savcılarla, Ahmet Yıldız isimli savcıyı özel atadınız, özel heyet belirlediniz, özel karar dikte ettirdiniz ve şu anda siyasete darbeye devam ediyorsunuz. Peki, buraları geçip bir dönem... Hani sizin itiraz edemeyeceğiniz bölüme geliyorum, özcesi şunu demeye çalışıyorum: Bu, dokuzuncu yargı paketi, artık 19 mu olur, 29 mu olur, umarız bu son yargı paketiniz olur, biz de kurtuluruz, Türkiye de kurtulur sizden; bunu diliyoruz. Ama diyelim yeni yargı paketleri olur, bu sözler belki size ışık tutar.

Erdoğan, bir dönem Başbakanken kadınlarla ilgili şöyle cümleleri vardı, ben aynen alıntılayacağım, soyadı meselesine geleceğim. "Bundan sonra her tecavüze uğrayan kadın, bunları ikna etmek için görüntü kaydıyla mı dolaşacak? Kocasından şiddet gören kadın, iddiasını ispat etmek için elinde kayıtla mı gelecek? Sokakta kurşunlanarak öldürülmüş kadın, cesedinin yanına görüntü kaydı mı koyacak? Ortada ifade tutanağı, adli tıp raporu, linç edilmiş bir kadın, darp edilmiş bir bebek var. Bunlar çıkmışlar, olayın nasıl olmadığını ispat etmeye çalışıyorlar; vicdan, vicdan, vicdan. Eğer o kadıncağız, başörtülü olmasaydı bu linç olur muydu? Dün başörtüsüne 'füruat' diyenler, bugün başörtülü bir kadın üzerinden başörtüsü düşmanlığını sürdürüyorlar." Bunlar tırnak içinde Cumhurbaşkanına ait. Şimdi kadın meselesini sadece başörtüsü meselesi olarak ele alıp yaşam hakkı dâhil bütün haklara, kadın haklarına bir saldırı var bu ülkede. İstanbul Sözleşmesi'nden çekilmek, kadınlar öldürülebilir demektir. O imzayı çekmek, kadınlara yönelik şiddetin, cinayetlerin meşrulaştırılması anlamına geliyor. İşte, bu konuşmayla ne kadar uyumlu, değil mi? Elinde kayıt olduğu hâlde, emniyete başvurduğu hâlde, elinde savcılığın uzaklaştırma kararı olduğu hâlde kaç kadın katledildi? Biz sayamıyoruz gerçekten. Benim kendi müvekkillerim, onlarca müvekkilim uzaklaştırma kararına rağmen katledildi, öldürüldü, kurtaramadık onları çünkü sistem bunu meşrulaştırıyor. Şimdi, en üstte yani iktidar pratiği bunu meşrulaştırıyor çünkü İstanbul Sözleşmesi'nden çıkan, kadına bakış açısını farklı şekillerde ifade eden, istediği kadın prototipini yaratmaya çalışan bir anlayış var. İşte, bizim reddettiğimiz tam da bu.

Şimdi, kaç çocuk cinsel saldırının mağduru oldu? Dün KEFEK'te de konuşulmuş; İpek Er'i katleden, tecavüz eden Musa Orhan korundu ya. Benim geçen dönem vekili olduğum Siirt'te o kadar çalıştık, mücadele ettik ki. Ya, bir asker bir çocuğa, genç kıza tecavüz ediyor, kadın ölüyor ama adam tutuklanmadı ya, tutuklanmadı. O kadın başörtülü olsaydı tutuklanırdı; işte, aradaki fark bu. Maalesef, maalesef, işte, kadınlar arasında da böyle ayrımcılık koyuyorsunuz ama İpek Er'in yanında, ölen maktulün yanında durup Musa Orhan'ı eleştiren Ezgi Mola'ya -bir sanatçı, sanat insanı, herkes tanır- ceza verildi, Twitter'da eleştirdi diye ya. Ya, bir tecavüzcüye... Bir de ceza verilmiş Musa Orhan'a, on yıldan fazla ceza da verildi, tutuklanmadı; eleştirdi diye Türkiye'nin tanıdığı bir sanat insanına ceza veriliyor ama katile, tecavüzcüye tutuklama bile yok. Bir gün cezaevinde tutturamadık, hemen bıraktılar çünkü ben Siirt'te bizzat takip ettim.

Şimdi, Musa Orhan'ı koruyan bir zihniyet var ve Türkiye'de kadınlar güvende değil, bunun sebebi de iktidardır çünkü kadın cinayetleri, kadına yönelik şiddet özel falan değil, politik. Biz bunu kadın hakları savunucuları olarak her yerde söyleriz, kadın meselesi politik bir meseledir. Ben ve eşim arasında değildir, sizin ve kız kardeşinizin arasında değildir, sistem bunu örer. Keşke bu konuda herkes biraz düşünse de dediklerimizi belki anlayabilir.

Şimdi, Cumhurbaşkanı Erdoğan ne demişti? "İspat etmek için kadınlar elinde kayıtla mı dolaşsın?" Katılıyorum buna, kayıtla mı dolaşalım ya? Gerçekten doğru söylemiş. Ya, kadın "Ben tecavüze uğradım." diye uğramadan önce video kaydı mı alsın yani? Ya da "Şiddet görüyorum ben evde. Ben eve bir video koyayım, şiddetimi ispatlayayım." mı desin? "Beyan esastır" derken tam da bunu söylüyoruz. Kadın "Ben şiddete uğradım." diye niye yalan atsın ya? Bir çocuk niye "Tecavüze uğradım." diye yalan atsın? Bilimsel, pedagojik raporlar bunun yalan olmadığını zaten söyler yani bunu takip edenler bilir.

NURETTİN ALAN (İstanbul) - Sayın Başkanım, konuyla alakası olmayan...

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Toparlıyorum Başkan.

NURETTİN ALAN (İstanbul) - Şimdi, söylenen doğru, yanlış olabilir de...

ŞENGÜL KARSLI (İstanbul) - 6284'ü tekrar ediyorsunuz, evet, doğru.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Neyi?

ŞENGÜL KARSLI (İstanbul) - 6284 sayılı Kanun'u. Kadının beyanının bir belge, delil aranmaksızın dikkate alındığını tekrar ettiniz.

NURETTİN ALAN (İstanbul) - Ya, o konu değil aslında, bu konuya gelirseniz...

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Niye siz benim konuşmama müdahale ediyorsunuz ya? Hakkınız yok.

NURETTİN ALAN (İstanbul) - Müdahale edeceğiz. Niye edeceğiz? Hiç alakasız şeyleri burada...

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Alakasız mı?

NURETTİN ALAN (İstanbul) - İç Tüzük'te yazıyor.

BAŞKAN CÜNEYT YÜKSEL - Sayın Alan, lütfen...

NURETTİN ALAN (İstanbul) - Hiç alakası olmayan... İç Tüzük'te de var, konuya dönmesi için uyarabilirsiniz Sayın Başkan.

BAŞKAN CÜNEYT YÜKSEL - Hatip konuşmasını yapsın, milletvekilimiz konuşmasını yapsın.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Sizin göreviniz burada sadece el kaldırmak çünkü.

NURETTİN ALAN (İstanbul) - Yok, hiç alakası yok. Bak, size bunu söylemek de görevim; bak, yapıyorum.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Benim görevim o değil; benim görevim, burada kadınları savunmak, burada halkı savunmak, tamam mı?

BAŞKAN CÜNEYT YÜKSEL - Sayın Alan...

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Ben senin gibi usulen oturmuyorum orada. Bileceksin, dinleyeceksin.

NURETTİN ALAN (İstanbul) - Dinliyoruz. Bakın, şunu söyleyeceğim: İç Tüzük'te yazan kurallara göre...

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Bana müdahale edemezsin, müdahale edemezsin.

NURETTİN ALAN (İstanbul) - Müdahale etmiyorum, Başkandan rica ediyorum, Başkana söyledim, size müdahale ettiğim yok.

BAŞKAN CÜNEYT YÜKSEL - Sayın Alan... Sayın Alan...

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Şimdi, soyadı bölümüne geldim, zaten tam "Toparlıyorum." dediğimde müdahale etti.

NURETTİN ALAN (İstanbul) - Ancak geldiniz, ancak geldiniz. Lütfen, buyurun, ne demek.

BAŞKAN CÜNEYT YÜKSEL - Evet, buyurun.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Şimdi, toparlayacak olursak...

NURETTİN ALAN (İstanbul) - Çok iyi olur.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Bu geneli üzerine, maddeler üzerine tabii ki konuşacağız.

BAŞKAN CÜNEYT YÜKSEL - Sizden sonra daha Gergerlioğlu bekliyor konuşmayı.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Vallahi, böyle ucube, böyle korkunç bir yasayı getirirsiniz haftalarca konuşuruz.

NURETTİN ALAN (İstanbul) - Ya, yasayı eleştirin, can kurban. Yasaya gelemediniz, saatlerce yasaya gelemiyorsunuz.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Yasaya geldim zaten.

NURETTİN ALAN (İstanbul) - Asıl sorun o, ben onu söylüyorum. İç Tüzük'e bağlı kalın.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Şimdi, bize ne diyorsunuz? Soyadına gelirsek, soyadı meselesinde diyorsunuz ki "Aile bütünlüğüne zarar verir." değil mi? Genel açıklamalar... Sabah da bazı konuşmalar oldu; işte, bir milletvekili mevkidaşımız dedi ki: "Ya, ne olacak? Çocuk baba soyadını mı alsın, anne soyadını mı? Öyle şey mi olur? Benim çocuğum benim soyadımı alsın, aile diye bir bütünlük var." Vallahi aileyi savunuyorum. Aile birliğini nasıl savunuyorum biliyor musunuz? Ailede eşler arasında eşitlik olur. Ailede eşler arasında saygı olur.

SİBEL SUİÇMEZ (Trabzon) - Anayasa'mız da öyle diyor.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Ailede eşler arasında ebeveynlik konusunda eşit haklar olur. Ailede şiddet olmaz. Ailede istismar olmaz. Ailede ensest olmaz. Ensest, şiddet, istismar, tecavüz, cinayet aile birliğine zarar vermiyor mu? Bunu önlemek için kılınızı kıpırdatmıyorsunuz ama soyadında "Soyadını değiştirelim." diyorsunuz.

NURETTİN ALAN (İstanbul) - Ya, atıyorsun, hiç alakası yok!

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Böyle bir şey olabilir mi ya? Böyle bir aile bütünlüğü koruma anlayışı olabilir mi? Siz soyadı... Bizim soyadımız, biziz ya, bizim benliğimiz. Biz kendi soyadımızla yaşamak istiyoruz.

NURETTİN ALAN (İstanbul) - En yüksek cezalar bizim dönemimizde verildi.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Şimdi, ailede eşitlik olmadan toplumsal bir eşitlik olamaz. Hani bu, genel bir doğrudur, ben burada söylemekten imtina etmiyorum. Önce ailede eşitlik olacak, sonra toplumsal eşitlik olacak.

BAŞKAN CÜNEYT YÜKSEL - Sayın Beştaş, suçlayıcı konuşmayalım lütfen.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Aile bütünlüğüne, aileye zararı işte demin anlattığım olgular veriyor; şiddet veriyor, tecavüz veriyor, hakaret veriyor, istismar veriyor, takip veriyor. Bunlarla mücadele edelim, biz varız, bununla mücadele ediyoruz.

BAŞKAN CÜNEYT YÜKSEL - Hepimiz aynı şeyi söylüyoruz.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Ama erkeğin burada soyadıyla erkeğin o sizin de imzanızın olduğu... Evet, 2005'ti galiba değil mi Medeni Kanun'un değiştiği, son hâli, reisliğin kaldırıldığı?

NURETTİN ALAN (İstanbul) - 2002, 2002; biz yokuz, bizden önce.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - 2002. Ha, siz yoktunuz. Sizden önce 2002'de "aile reisliği" kavramı kaldırıldı. Bu Soyadı Kanunu dayatmasıyla reislik getiriliyor aslında. Erkek tekrar evin reisi ilan ediliyor, "Soyadıyla bu mesele devam etsin." deniliyor.

Kadınların tabii ki emeği de çalınıyor, zihinleri hapsedilmek isteniyor. Açıkçası, Anayasa ve uluslararası sözleşmelere göre -söylemiş olayım- kadınlar ve erkekler eşit ama siz bunu tanımıyorsunuz, evlilikte de boşanmada da eşit hak ve sorumluluklara sahipler. Bu nedenle, erkeğe tanınan -soyadına- çocuğunun soyadını belirleme hakkı kadına da tanınmalı ya.

Bu soy meselesi, erkek egemenliği meselesini biz temelden reddediyoruz ve bu konuda kadınlar olarak biz aileyi soyadıyla özdeşleştirmiyoruz. Biz soyadına erkek egemen perspektifle bakmıyoruz. Sonuçta, isteyen istediği soyadını kullansın. Bu aman aman dünyanın soyadı da değil. Tabii ki buradaki amaç sadece soyadı meselesi değil, buradaki amaç sizin toplumu dizayn etme isteğiniz, arzunuz ve kadının kimliğini, kadının iradesini tanımama yaklaşımınızdır.
records.englishTranslation:
MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Thank you, Mr. President.

Yes, what can we say about it in general? Frankly, especially in Türkiye, we have been through the processes of harmonization with the European Union laws since the beginning of 1999, that is, we know that there were serious developments and legal changes in the field of rights and freedoms at that time, but unfortunately, due to many developments in the world in general and in the Middle East, security policies led by the United States began to be prioritized. In other words, this is not just for Turkey, but in fact such a trend has begun globally, and as security has taken precedence over freedom and discussions on this issue have intensified both in Türkiye and in the world, unfortunately security has become a sacred value in some countries, systems and regimes. In other words, not freedom, but security: "Let our leading security be ensured." Of course, we know that there is such a theory, a movement, a debate, and even today - in quotation marks - the security concern is "Personal freedom, rights, democracy or security?" The debate still maintains its existence very vividly.

Now, in Türkiye - there are those who remember - in our country, in 2006, many changes were made to the Anti-Terrorism Law restricting rights and freedoms, and Türkiye actually opted for security policies. I want to remind you of that period again, but I remember this very well: The Turkish Penal Code changed in 2005, we followed this exactly during my years as a lawyer, the Criminal Procedure Code changed; In fact - you are a party that came to power in 2002 and those who are making this offer now, of course, have that history and that acquis - revealed its perspective on these freedoms at that time.

Then, of course, there is a gap between the founding philosophy of the ruling party, the Justice and Development Party, its founding claims, and today - I don't know if the concept is compatible with it, but let me say "cliff", I don't know any other concept in Turkish; If you want, I can say it in Kurdish, there are these concepts there too - so there is an incredible angle. "I will abolish tutelage in the judiciary and bring freedom. I will end poverty, lies and prohibitions." It entered with a flourish with the slogans of "3Y", "democracy" and "freedom", but the destruction currently created in the field of law is so great that it almost takes another decades of work to return. In other words, the Constitutional amendment regarding the introduction of the Presidential system of government - I have been in the Parliament since 2015, we also made that Constitutional amendment with some friends, they are here too - and the subsequent judicial packages, obviously I will not count them, but - I will not count the ones before the Presidential system of government - this is the ninth judicial package. Here, our Commission President just said, "Wait for the tenth judicial package." He said, there is always a backside, there is always tomorrow. In other words, it is not over under the AKP government, it is not solved, but we are experiencing this together in the Parliament and the General Assembly; Let's get it out of here, it comes before us again in the next bill. Well, for example, I remember very well - it was a few years ago - a legal regulation was brought before us that introduced trustees and appointed trustees to municipalities, and all hell broke loose. I was the Deputy Chairman of the Group at the General Assembly - I just handed it over, it's been a few months - they came immediately - in a hurry - the ruling executives said, "Of course we are suffering from this, this is not right, you know, we cannot accept it either." said those in the group management at that time. Then, a few months later, they included the trustee in the decree and the trustee usurpation and coup is still continuing. Now, we are faced with such a practice of power.

Now, frankly, if we ask globally what happened with these judicial packages, everyone can be labeled, everyone can be a terrorist; very natural. So now, if you call someone a "terrorist", they don't object at all, because if you are not on the side of the government, you are a terrorist, that is, it is very easy, and the bad part is that it is very easy in front of the judiciary. There are also judicial members from the Supreme Court and the Council of State here, so after all, we are also following these decisions, this system, closely. There is an authoritarian system. You brought something crazy called "multiple bar system", now we are talking about allowances. So frankly, the government said, "Let me establish a bar association close to me." he said. "There should be bar associations that I can manage, there should be bar associations that I can transfer money to. I have created the mechanisms to determine judges, prosecutors, the Supreme Court, the Council of State, the HSK, the Constitutional Court, all the higher judicial institutions and members of the local judiciary, but I cannot interfere with lawyers." he said; I'm saying it literally. What about getting involved with lawyers? Of course he will intervene in bar associations. Bar associations number 2 were established as new bar associations that could shape bar association management. All hell broke loose in Türkiye, hundreds of thousands of lawyers took to the streets, but the government did not take a single step back and the new bar associations registered their own staff, such as their own managers, candidates, parliamentary candidates, former mayors, to those bar associations, and now we are living with multiple bar associations. In fact, they wanted to deal a blow to both the defense and the lawyers, and it was done.

Now, regarding this, again on the issue of inequality, with the judicial package, you brought such a package in the past years that an amnesty was given to gangs, rape criminals and abusers, in other words, a tacit amnesty was brought to us. Well, the man who killed his wife was released, then he went out and shot his wife again. The man who was arrested for child rape was released again and committed the same crime again. The gang members - we have examples of these, don't shake your head like that, we are actively following them, don't shake your head literally - were left behind. Let's say that you get your wish, in fairness, not a single regulation was made to the opponents. No regulations have been made for journalists, democratic institutions, civil society, lawyers, human rights defenders, women's rights defenders, and the execution rate for political crimes is still three out of four. An unequal execution law is in force and is maintained. We insisted and stubbornly said: Ensure equality in execution, so that everyone is equal. So, one commits "a" crime, the other commits "b" crime - I say crime in quotation marks - why are there separate execution institutions? Moreover, the one who speaks, writes, writes, and engages in politics; You imprisoned someone like Demirtaş, a person whom the overwhelming majority of Türkiye - without your pressure and pressure - accepts as a leader, just because he is an opponent of Erdoğan, just because he is successful, just because he is sympathetic, just because he is supported; Is there any other explanation for this? Osman Kavala is still inside, Figen Yüksekdağ is still inside; You staged a coup against our party just for this reason, to silence the opposition, and the effects of this coup continue, and are still being perpetrated, because our friends' hostage situation continues. Unfortunately, the judiciary is the most basic instrument, the most basic stick of the government in this regard. They keep everyone in line with the threat of judiciary, for example, we all go to the field, they say: Yes, we are taking photos, people want to take photos with their representatives. I came across it a few times, believe me, I was very upset. They say: "I love you very much, but I cannot share the photo because my child will be a civil servant, if they see our photo with you, he is a DEM PARTY deputy..."

SİBEL SUİÇMEZ (Trabzon) - We say it ourselves, not to withdraw.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - "What should I do, my child is a police officer." they say or "My child is in the military." they say. I come across it a lot, he takes photos and says "Keep it private, but I won't share it on my social media." he says, "We don't share it anyway." we say. You have created such a society of fear because an investigation may be launched and a lawsuit may be filed against him. "Why did you take a photo with a member of parliament from DEM, CHP or TİP?" saying.

Now, here the HSK has already - what can I say - become the guardian of the judiciary and the government. We only express the principle of separation of powers with such sentences, there is no such separation. In this country, the President and the spokesmen of the government in this country are pointing at, threatening and blackmailing the Council of State, the Supreme Court and the Constitutional Court; He does this openly to the press. We have seen how the Presidents of the Council of State collect tea and make statements, I am not counting the universities anyway. I am not making reference to members of the judiciary or anyone else here, I am making a general assessment. For this reason, there is no principle of separation of powers, that is, today at the Supreme Court of Appeals, Deputy Minister of Internal Affairs Muhterem Bey - we were working together - left within a week - friends, I think it was within a week, right - he left, was elected, and was immediately made a member of the higher judiciary. İrfan Fidan remained a member of the Supreme Court of Appeals for a week - it could be a week or 15 days, there are no figures, I am speaking extemporaneously - then he became a member of the Constitutional Court. This could be a joke! He did not even sign a petition as a member of the Supreme Court, it was sent to the Constitutional Court as a project; I'm just mentioning two names. Now, will they go beyond the government's word? If it were me, I wouldn't go out.

ADEM YILDIRIM (Istanbul) - Now "Obey his decision." you say.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - But look, as I said, "I am very sorry, abide by this decision." I say.

NURETTİN ALAN (Istanbul) - So they were good guys.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - No, I am speaking consistently, I even said that.

ADEM YILDIRIM (Istanbul) - OK but...

ÖMER FARUK GERGERLİOĞLU (Kocaeli) - That's what he said, though.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - I said that, I said, "Even saying this, I feel sad." So I know what I'm saying.

Now, efforts are already being made to silence the Parliament, internal regulations are constantly being discussed; let's see what happens. The Constitution is suspended with this proposal, we know it, it is obvious. Is this a process of destruction or not? I think it's a process of destruction. You are trying to destroy directly, you are trying to destroy an order, that is, the existing legal system. Social engineering is being carried out and the ruling party is literally working as a demolition team, it also works as a team to overthrow the law.

Now, of course, let me say this: If you gave it to a mold maker, I swear the society would design it better. He wouldn't bother with so many packages, he would do it all at once; And you cannot do this either. I swear, I am not very good at saying harsh words to such people's faces, but I swear you are incompetent. I swear to God, you are incompetent even when setting up your own system. You can't do it with one package, you can't do it with two. One day you bring it, the next day you bring something new. “Oh, we forgot about that, let's do this.” You say, "Oh, this place didn't work, let's change this place." you say. I mean, I don't know how many times a law you've been making for nine years has changed, we already give you the numbers.

ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - In other words, we do not have to accept the insults of everyone who talks about the article, Mr. President.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - I am not insulting.

ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - Everyone in this Commission is our colleague, we are lawyers. This style of speech may be General Assembly style, but this is the Commission.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - I am not insulting. I don't insult, I don't insult.

The Commission, like the General Assembly, is a part of the legislative activity. This is a part of the legislative activity, we are carrying out legislative activities here. We are not chatting on the street here, we are discussing a law and you cannot object like that.

ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - Speaking as you please cannot be considered as Commission work.

FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) - You cannot speak in street language.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - With what mouth?

FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) - You will not speak in street language here.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - You are speaking in street language, I know very well what I am saying.

FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) - You cannot answer in street language.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - "You are speaking in street language." You can't say.

FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) - You say "incompetent".

MAYOR CÜNEYT Yüksel - Dear representatives, please...

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - You have brought the law before us with street words. You brought the law before us.

CHAIRMAN CÜNEYT YÜKSEL - Mr. Tuğrul... Mr. Karslı, Mr. Tuğrul, ask for a word and we will give it that way.

FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) - Do not insult...

ZÜLKÜF UÇAR (Van) - You are the one speaking in street language. No insults, nothing.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - I insulted, with what concept did I insult? What concept did I insult?

MAYOR CÜNEYT Yüksel - Mr. Beştaş...

ZÜLKÜF UÇAR (Van) - You will try to pass the bill and you will not be patient with the use of three words, that's fine!

ORHAN KIRCALI (Samsun) - Please friends...

MAYOR CÜNEYT YÜKSEL - Mr. Beştaş, continue.

FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) - We are not your whippersnappers here.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Of course you are. You are in power, you will suffer.

MAYOR CÜNEYT YÜKSEL - Mr. Beştaş, please continue.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - You are in power, you will listen. You are trying to design society. Power...

ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - You cannot insult.

MAYOR CÜNEYT Yüksel - Mr. Karslı...

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - I am not insulting.

FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) - You are insulting.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Look, I'm not insulting.

FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) - What does "incompetent" mean?

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - If I did it, "I did it." I say.

FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) - If I shouted "Incompetent" at you now...

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - You are incompetent! This is not an insult.

FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) - Then you are the incompetent.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - I say you are worse, but at least you are incompetent.

FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) - Then you are the one who is incompetent.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - I said that ironically.

ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - You can explain your purpose with appropriate words.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - I swear to God, you have turned the country into a puzzle board.

MAYOR CÜNEYT Yüksel - It is understood, it is understood.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - You turned the laws into a puzzle. Currently, a new lawyer will not be able to make a defense under the same law after two years, will not be able to write the same petition, and will not know which article is in which law. Young people graduating from law school every day have difficulty keeping up with new laws. Yes, this is incompetence.

NURETTİN ALAN (Istanbul) - What do you mean, shouldn't we enact a new law?

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Yes, this is not knowing. If you consider this an insult, then go for it. Do whatever you want.

FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) - Then you are the incompetent one.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - What?

FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) - Then you are the incompetent one.

SİBEL SUİÇMEZ (Trabzon) - Even though she is doing it, she is incompetent.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - I swear how much we have...

SİBEL SUİÇMEZ (Trabzon) - Are you the one who makes the law because you are incompetent...

ORHAN KIRCALI (Samsun) - OK sir, here you go.

MAYOR CÜNEYT Yüksel - Yes, Mr. Beştaş...

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Oh, I said, "You are authoritarian." I say, "You are practicing fascism." I say, "There is authoritarianism, totalitarian regime." I say, but he doesn't get offended, "You are incompetent." I say, you are offended. Oh my, how sensitive you are. You really are racist. I say, you never get offended.

FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) - We are racists, huh?

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Yes.

FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) - Are you saying this?

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Yes, that's exactly what we say.

MAYOR CÜNEYT Yüksel - Let's not talk to each other, please.

Mr. Beştaş, let's sum it up.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - President, if you intervene, we will not talk to each other.

MAYOR CÜNEYT Yüksel - Here you go.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Now, here you are changing the same law ten times. For example, the law touched you after December 17-25, what happened? Then you changed it immediately. I remember this very well: You passed laws that would be implemented for three months before July 15, with the judicial package; You evacuated the people inside, you established a new court, new court, high criminal courts; Then, when you were done with that work, you threw those courts into disrepair. We experienced this. This is not a secret thing. The legal system of this country has now become your puzzle board, and when it comes to your benefit, you say, "Wow, we defend democracy, we are democrats. There is no one above us." On top of that, you have a strange habit of expecting thanks. I still don't understand that, both in the Parliament and in the commissions, "Well, we did good things too." Either do good things and let's thank you ten times a day. That's why we defend them, that's what we fight for. So our aim is not to criticize for the sake of criticizing. Why do we work so hard here, why do we work so hard? Don't we have any work to do? We are trying to do a job for the benefit of this country and we are representatives of the people like you.

Now, however - as you know, you were angry at the incompetence in this matter - you have mastered this subject, as the President says, "Our apprenticeship period is over, our mastery period has begun." You have truly become masters at changing and evolving the laws to suit yourself, and at imposing the ideology, belief, and identity you want. You are introducing laws with such beautiful maneuvers that you are doing this in recent times.

Now, yes, you are continuing the coups with greater counter-coups. Currently, the AKP and MHP government is a coupist government. Why is he a coup plotter? Because he maintains his power by keeping politicians in prison. Because women's rights... It is also a government that covets the gains gained from decades of struggle and staged a coup, and unfortunately you always keep the coup dynamic alive.

Now, you were criticizing September 12 ten years ago, you had very good statements, so they are still in the media. You were trying to erase the traces of the so-called September 12 junta, now we suddenly found you almost more authoritarian than the September 12 supporters. Well, it turned into an authoritarian regime. Supposedly, "We will remove the tutelage centers and end them." You were saying that you found a super tutelage institution like trusteeship; my trustee! Think about it, ask a 7-year-old, 3-year-old or 5-year-old child about democracy and they will say, "It is the right of the people to choose their own ruler." says. It elects its manager in an association, in a foundation, in a bar association, in the medical chamber, but the largest one is of course the Parliament and the President at the moment. You are canceling the people's choice. This is exactly what we call tutelage. And with that, of course, what do you build it with? With cruelty. You know no limits in cruelty and you have truly become the new owner of the tutelage system; We can congratulate you on this matter.

So what will happen? In other words, there is almost no one left in the political arena that you would not call a "terrorist" unless of course you are one of them. "Disgrace" and "pit" are these concepts and now because I say you are incompetent, "You insulted us." you say.

FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) - Of course, we will call the terrorist "terrorist", why not?

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Well, call the terrorist "terrorist", I swear.

FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) - Of course, we will call the terrorist "terrorist".

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - I think you should call the terrorist "terrorist".

FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) - That's what we say.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - But you cannot call someone else a "terrorist" while you are committing terrorism.

FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) - We call terrorists "terrorists".

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - You can't say that.

FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) - You know what the terrorists are doing in the mountains, you know very well.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Oh, we know, we know.

FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) - You know very well what those terrorists did in the mountains.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - We know how you work hand in hand with coup plotters and terrorists, and we know how to respond to you.

FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) - We know, we know very well who is a terrorist.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - President, you are probably allowing his intervention on purpose, aren't you? I am asking you as the Chairman of the Commission.

MAYOR CÜNEYT YÜKSEL - Mr. Beştaş, please continue.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Tell him, not me.

FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) - When we talk, you interfere in everything.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Don't interrupt me.

Are there any parties or associations left that have not been declared terrorists? No; There is no union left. Now, in this totalitarian regime, there is no such thing as a check-and-balance mechanism. Now there is a Parliament, and you are trying to suppress it with this method. Even here, there are almost attempts to establish tutelage over our MPs; a tool of oppression. Why should we be afraid of you? At most, you will send us to prison like our friends. You gave instructions to the prosecutors, dozens, hundreds of summaries of proceedings were prepared for all of us. 70 percent of the sentence given to Yüksekdağ is his speeches, freedom of thought and expression. So, at most, you will do this, and the world sees this, which means that it again gets in the way of all of us, and most of all, your cruelty will be exposed; Let's see this too.

Now, on the other hand, let's not discuss the judiciary too much, but I would like to say that the Kobani conspiracy case is a litmus test. With the prosecutors you gave instructions, you appointed a special prosecutor named Ahmet Yıldız, appointed a special delegation, dictated a special decision, and now you are continuing to blow politics. Well, let's go past this and come to the part that you cannot object to, in short, I am trying to say this: This is the ninth judicial package, will it be 19 or 29, we hope this will be your last judicial package, we will be saved too, Türkiye will be saved from you too; We wish this. But let's say there are new judicial packages, maybe these words will shed light on you.

Erdoğan, when he was Prime Minister for a while, said the following about women: I will quote them verbatim and come to the surname issue. "From now on, will every woman who was raped go around with a video recording to convince them? Will the woman who was subjected to violence by her husband come with a recording in her hand to prove her claim? Will the woman who was shot to death on the street put a video recording next to her corpse? There is a statement report, a forensic report, a lynched woman, a battered baby. They have come out and are trying to prove how the incident did not happen; conscience, conscience, conscience. If that woman had not been wearing a headscarf, would this lynching have happened? Yesterday. "Those who call the headscarf 'furuat' today continue their hostility towards the headscarf through a woman wearing a headscarf." These belong to the President in quotation marks. Now, by treating the women's issue only as a headscarf issue, there is an attack on all rights and women's rights, including the right to life. Withdrawing from the Istanbul Convention means women can be killed. Signing that signature means legitimizing violence and murders against women. Well, this fits in well with the conversation, doesn't it? How many women were murdered even though they had a record, even though they applied to the police, even though they had a restraining order from the prosecutor's office? We really can't count. My own clients, dozens of them, were murdered despite the restraining order, we could not save them because the system legitimizes this. Now, at the top, the practice of power legitimizes this because there is an understanding that comes out of the Istanbul Convention, expresses its perspective on women in different ways, and tries to create the female prototype it wants. This is exactly what we reject.

Now, how many children have been victims of sexual assault? It was also discussed in KEFEK yesterday; Musa Orhan, who murdered and raped İpek Er, was protected. We worked and fought so hard in Siirt, where I was the deputy last term. Well, a soldier rapes a child, a young girl, the woman dies, but the man was not arrested, he was not arrested. If that woman had been wearing a headscarf, she would have been arrested; Well, that's the difference. Unfortunately, unfortunately, this is how you discriminate among women, but Ezgi Mola - an artist, a person of art, everyone knows her - who stood next to İpek Er, next to the deceased, and criticized Musa Orhan, was punished, just because she criticized him on Twitter. What about a rapist... Musa Orhan was also sentenced to more than ten years, but he was not arrested; An artist known to Türkiye is being punished for criticizing him, but there is not even an arrest for a murderer or rapist. One day we couldn't get it to work in prison, they released it immediately because I personally followed it up in Siirt.

Now, there is a mentality that protects Musa Orhan and women are not safe in Türkiye, the reason for this is the government because femicides and violence against women are not private, they are political. As women's rights advocates, we say this everywhere; the women's issue is a political issue. It is not between me and my wife, it is not between you and your sister, the system creates this. I wish everyone would think a little about this and maybe understand what we are saying.

Now, what did President Erdoğan say? "Should women walk around with a recording to prove it?" I agree with this, should we go around with a record? He was indeed right. Well, the woman says, "I was raped." So, should he take a video recording before stopping by? Or "I see violence at home. Let me post a video at home to prove my violence." should you say? This is exactly what we mean when we say "Declaration is essential". Woman: "I was subjected to violence." Why would he lie? Why does a child say "I was raped?" Why should he lie? Scientific and pedagogical reports already say that this is not a lie, so those who follow this know.

NURETTİN ALAN (Istanbul) - Mr. President, this has nothing to do with the subject...

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - I'm wrapping up, Mayor.

NURETTİN ALAN (Istanbul) - Now, what is said may be true or false...

ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - You are repeating 6284, yes, that is correct.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - What?

ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - Law No. 6284. You repeated that the woman's statement was taken into consideration without seeking any documents or evidence.

NURETTİN ALAN (Istanbul) - Well, it's not actually that topic, if you come to this topic...

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Why are you interfering with my speech? You have no right.

NURETTİN ALAN (Istanbul) - We will intervene. Why will we? Unrelated things here...

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Is it irrelevant?

NURETTİN ALAN (Istanbul) - It is written in the Internal Regulations.

MAYOR CÜNEYT Yüksel - Dear Alan, please...

NURETTİN ALAN (Istanbul) - It has nothing to do with it... It is also in the Internal Regulations, you can warn him to return to the subject, Mr. President.

MAYOR CÜNEYT YÜKSEL - Let the orator make his speech, let our parliamentarian make his speech.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Because your duty here is just to raise your hand.

NURETTİN ALAN (Istanbul) - No, it has nothing to do with it. Look, it's my duty to tell you this; Look, I'm doing it.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - That is not my duty; My job is to defend women here, to defend the people here, okay?

CHAIRMAN CÜNEYT Yüksel - Mr. Alan...

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - I do not sit there as usual like you. You will know, you will listen.

NURETTİN ALAN (Istanbul) - We are listening. Look, I will say this: According to the rules written in the Bylaws...

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - You cannot interfere with me, you cannot intervene.

NURETTİN ALAN (Istanbul) - I am not interfering, I ask the President, I told the President, I am not interfering with you.

CHAIRMAN CÜNEYT Yüksel - Dear Alan... Dear Alan...

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Now, I have come to the surname section, and I am already "tidying it up." When I said that, he intervened.

NURETTİN ALAN (Istanbul) - But you came, you came. Please, please, what does it mean?

MAYOR CÜNEYT Yüksel - Yes, please.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Now, to summarize...

NURETTİN ALAN (Istanbul) - It would be very good.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Of course, we will talk about this in general and about the items.

MAYOR CÜNEYT Yüksel - After you, Gergerlioğlu is waiting to speak.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - I swear, you bring such a freak, such a terrible law, we will talk about it for weeks.

NURETTİN ALAN (Istanbul) - Oh, criticize the law, life is sacrificed. You couldn't come to the law, you couldn't come to the law for hours.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - I have already come to the law.

NURETTİN ALAN (Istanbul) - That's the real problem, that's what I'm saying. Adhere to the Bylaws.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Now, what do you say to us? As for the surname, you say that the surname issue "damages the unity of the family." isn't it? General statements... There were some conversations in the morning; Well, one of our parliamentary counterparts said: "So, what will happen? Should the child take the father's surname or the mother's surname? Is such a thing possible? My child should take my surname, there is an integrity called family." I swear I defend the family. Do you know how I defend family unity? There is equality between spouses in the family. There is respect between spouses in the family.

SİBEL SUİÇMEZ (Trabzon) - Our Constitution also says so.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - In the family, spouses have equal rights regarding parenthood. There is no violence in the family. There is no abuse in the family. There is no incest in the family. Don't incest, violence, abuse, rape and murder damage the family unity? You don't move a muscle to prevent this, but your surname says "Let's change your surname." you say.

NURETTİN ALAN (Istanbul) - Oh, you throw it away, it has nothing to do with it!

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Could such a thing happen? Is it possible to have such an understanding of protecting family integrity? You surname... Our surname, we are, our identity. We want to live with our own surname.

NURETTİN ALAN (Istanbul) - The highest penalties were given during our period.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Now, there cannot be social equality without equality in the family. Well, this is a general truth, I do not hesitate to say it here. First there will be equality in the family, then there will be social equality.

MAYOR CÜNEYT YÜKSEL - Mr. Beştaş, please let's not talk accusatory.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - The facts I have just explained are the damage to family integrity and family; It inflicts violence, inflicts rape, inflicts insults, inflicts abuse, inflicts stalking. Let's fight against these, we are here, we are fighting against this.

MAYOR CÜNEYT Yüksel - We all say the same thing.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - But the man's surname is here, and that is your signature... Yes, I think it was 2005, wasn't it, when the Civil Code was changed, its final version, the headship was abolished?

NURETTİN ALAN (Istanbul) - 2002, 2002; We do not exist, before us.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - 2002. Oh, you weren't there. Before you, the concept of "head of family" was abolished in 2002. In fact, chieftainship is imposed by the imposition of this Surname Law. The man is declared the head of the house again, "Let this issue continue with his surname." It is said.

Of course, women's labor is also stolen, and their minds are tried to be imprisoned. Frankly, according to the Constitution and international agreements - let me tell you - women and men are equal, but you do not recognize this, they have equal rights and responsibilities in marriage and divorce. For this reason, the right given to men to determine the surname of their child should also be granted to women.

We fundamentally reject this issue of lineage and male dominance, and as women, we do not identify the family with the surname. We do not look at surnames from a male-dominated perspective. After all, anyone can use whatever surname they want. This is not the last name of the world. Of course, the purpose here is not just the surname issue, the purpose here is your desire and desire to design society and your approach not to recognize the identity of women and the will of women.

records.originalTurkish:
MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Teşekkürler Sayın Başkan.
Ben de herkesi saygıyla selamlıyorum.
Evet, yine bir yargı paketi, yine Komisyon salonundayız ve yine daha önce yapılan değişiklikler üzerinden kaçıncı kezdir geçiyoruz. Yargı paketleri, paketleri... Yani son on yılı bilen bir yerden bunu söylüyorum, bilen arkadaşlar da var ve maalesef şu anda Türkiye'nin en temel gündemlerinden biri infaz paketi oldu bir anda. Neden? Çünkü bir beklenti yaratıldı ve bundan öncesinde tabii ki içinde yaşadığımız siyasal iklim çok önemli. Uzun yıllar sonra bir
barış ve demokratik toplum talebi vücut bulmuş hâlde ve aslında yüksek sesle ifade eden, etmeyen herkeste artık bu barışın tesis edilmesini, artık Türkiye'de hukukun üstünlüğünün geçerli olmasını, artık demokrasinin ekmek kadar, su kadar büyük bir ihtiyaç olduğu konusunda aslında geniş bir görüş birliği oluşmuş durumda. Sürekli sahada olan bir arkadaşınız olarak söylüyorum, gittiğimiz her yerde -ekonomi tabii ki önemli, yaşam koşulları tabii ki önemli, kadın cinayetleri tabii ki önemli ama- ilk sorulan soru "Bu süreç nasıl olacak?" Devam ediyor "Umarız nihayete erer, umarız bu çatışmalı ortam biter, umarız bu ayrımcılık biter ve tabii ki umarız bu demokrasi tam anlamıyla vücut bulur." Şimdi, böyle bir zaman diliminde bu infaz paketinin, yargı paketinin bu şekilde ayrımcı ve eşitsiz bir içerikle gelmesi önemli; asıl tartışılan mesele bu. Tabii ki bu süreçte, Sayın Bahçeli'nin çağrısı, Sayın Öcalan'ın bu konuda 27 Şubat açıklaması ve sonrasında yapılan bütün açıklamalarda ortada tabii ki pazarlık yok. Doğru, biz de bunu defaatle ifade ediyoruz. Ortada bir al-ver süreci yok, bir pazarlık yok ama ne var? Barışın tesisi ve tabii ki demokrasinin inşası var, demokratik bir toplumu hep birlikte yaratmak var. Şart yok, pazarlık yok ama gereklilikler var. Bazı meselelerde şart olmasına gerek yok yani neticede silahlar olmayacaksa, farklı bir iklime uyanacaksak şu anda yasalarda var olan bu ayrımcı uygulamaların da son bulması gerekiyor; bu bir gereklilik, altını çizerek söylüyorum. Bu infaz paketine bakarken ilk ne hissettim? İnanın, ilkin Kürt meselesini gördüm; yine Kürt'e hukuk yok, yine demokrasi yok. Ha, sadece Kürt'e değil Kürt'ün yanında durana da dostu olana da muhalefet edene de yok yani bunu da altını çizerek söyleyeyim. Birazdan açacağım ne kastettiğimi. Çok yargı paketi tartışmalarında bulundum. Uyum yasalarını anlatmayacağım, Adalet ve Kalkınma Partisinin 2002'den sonra "Avrupa Birliğine uyum" adı altında attığı ileri adımlara atıfta bulunarak söylüyorum. Sonra, 2007'de tekrar geri adımlar atıldı PVSK'de değişiklikler yapıldı, tekrar güvenlik meselesi 11 Eylülden sonra bütün özgürlüklerin önüne geçti yani bunu yakın tarih açısından hep birlikte yaşadık maalesef ve TMK tartışmaları Türkiye'de hiç bitmedi aslında. Terörle Mücadele Kanunu niye var, niye cezaları artırıyor, düşünce ve ifade özgürlüğü neden suç olmaya devam ediyor; neden her söz, her etkinlik, her demokratik tepki -tırnak içinde- terörle ilişkilendiriliyor? Bu tartışmalar yeni değil yani bizler için son on yılları kapsıyor ve maalesef, ifade özgürlüğü hep sorun olmaya devam etti ve Türkiye ne yazık ki Avrupa İnsan Hakları Mahkemesi önünde -sayı vermeyeceğim- ifade özgürlüğü konusunda çok sayıda, sayısız ihlal kararıyla muhatap olan bir ülke konumunda. Ben avukatlık yaptığım yıllarda Avrupa İnsan Hakları mahkemesine çok başvuru yapan birisi olarak da o dava dosyalarını kısmen bilirim yani hangi maddelerden nelerin ihlal kapsamına girdiğini. Bu TMK'nin etkisi artık öyle bir hâle geldi ki alelade bir yurttaş toplumsal bir gösteriye katılması ya da herhangi bir konuda açıklama yapmış olması, birisinin yanında durmuş olması, bir görselin arkasında olması gibi sebeplerle örgüt üyeliğinden, örgüt adına suç işlemekten ceza alıyor, hâlâ da alıyorlar yani bu eski değil, bunlar devam eden uygulamalar. Yani gazeteciler sadece örgütle ilgili bir haber yaptığı için tutuklanabiliyor ve maalesef, bu devam ediyor. Özcesi, TMK düşünce ve ifade özgürlüğü konusunda tüm hakları, temel hakları ihlal ediyor ve bu hâlâ
devam ediyor. Aslında Anayasa'ya aykırı Terörle Mücadele Kanunu fakat bir yasa olarak düzenlendi ve yapılan değişikliklerle hakların ve özgürlüklerin önünde TMK bir duvar gibi âdeta yükseldi ve maalesef bu duvar devam ediyor.
Sanırım, burada önemli oranda hukukçular olarak bulunuyoruz. Mesela, 3713'te düzenlenen hiçbir suç aslında 5237 sayılı TCK'de cezasız değil, hepsi var yani bütün suçlar tanımlanmış. Yine, özel soruşturma usulleri 5271'de, Ceza Muhakemesi Kanunu'nda var fakat nedense ayrıca ek bir dikişe, halk diliyle çift dikiş hâline getiriliyor ve baskı artırılıyor. Bugünkü önümüzdeki paket de dâhil, TMK hariç. TMK demek ne demek? Kürtler hariç diyorlar. Kürtlerle ilgili, Kürt meselesiyle ilgili yaptığınız her konuşma, her söz, her fiil, her siyasi etkinlik ya da siyaset yapma TMK kapsamında değerlendiriliyor. Maalesef, bugün işte en çirkin suçları işleyenler, toplumda aslında dışlanan, kabul edilmeyen insanlar Covid yasasıyla pandemi döneminde serbest kaldılar. O zaman çok güçlü bir muhalefet yapmıştık, siz siyasi sebeplerle içeride olan insanlara ölüm emri veriyorsunuz demiştik. İşte, şu anda bütün bu konuştuklarımızı o zaman da ifade etmiştik ve maalesef haklı çıktık. Ne oluyor? İşte, tacizden, tecavüzden, hırsızlıktan, çetecilikten, uyuşturucudan suç işleyenler Covid'den yararlandı ama Kobani davasından arkadaşlarımız hâlâ cezaevindeler ve galiba bir yıl on gün oldu, daha artıyor süre, hâlâ gerekçeli karar yazılmadı. Sadece örnek olarak söylüyorum. Bu Kobani davası ki içinde bütün MYK üyelerimiz o dönemde ve önceki dönem Eş Genel Başkanlarımız Sevgili Figen Yüksekdağ ve Selahattin Demirtaş da hâlâ 9'uncu yılında, beş ay sonra dokuz yılları dolacak, cezaevindeler. Dokuz yıl demek ne kadar kolay, değil mi? Dokuz yıl yani özgürlüklerinden yoksunlar ve tek bir -tırnak içinde- suçları var: Siyaset yapmak, muhalefet etmek.
Yine, o dönemde çıkan bütün kanunlarda TMK hariç, Kürtler hariç, muhalifler hariç kanunlar çıkarıldı, düzenlendi ve bugün, işte "Covid düzenlemesi eşit uygulansın." dedik, iktidar sözcülerinin yaptıkları açıklamalarda büyük bir beklenti oluştu gerçekten. Sadece ayrımcılığa uğrayanlar değil gerçekten sadece Covid sebebiyle dışarıda olup tekrar cezaevine gitmesi gereken de on binlerce insan var, bir de uygulanması gereken, TMK kapsamındaki şüpheliler de var. Hiçbiri konulmadı pakete ve şu anda gerçek bir infial hâli var cezaevlerinde, telefonlarımız susmuyor, mesaj kutularımız gerçekten boşalmıyor. Bununla ilgili kanun teklifinde mesela her zaman, her yerde ve herkes için suç oluşturan yağma, hırsızlık -tekrar edeyim- kasten yaralama, örgüt kurmak gibi suçlardan indirim öngörülüyor ama şiddet unsuru içermeyen, tamamen düşünce ifade özgürlüğü kapsamında ya da demokratik tepki kapsamındaki fiiller dava dosyaları sebebiyle maalesef kapsam dışı bırakılıyor bunlar. İşte, ayrımcılığı daha nasıl tarif edebiliriz? Onu da bilmiyorum. Açık bir eşitsizlik hâli var.
Hukuk fakültelerinde hepimiz öğrendik, kanunlar genel olur, soyut olur, o kanunu okuduğunuzda kimler için geldiğini, kim için geldiğini bilemezsiniz ama bizde artık kanunlar kişiye göre, kimliklere göre, inançlara göre, cinsiyetlere göre tasnif edilebiliyor. Ben Feti Bey'in söylediği uygulamayı ayrıca değerlendireceğim, doğru, uygulamada da sorun var ama bir de kanunun kendisinde, özünde zaten ayrımcılık var, özünde. Uygulayıcının eline gitmesine gerek yok. Şimdi, düzenleme yapıyoruz, diyoruz ki: "Şu, şu, şu maddeler hariç." Artık onu söylemeye bile gerek duyulmuyor "TMK kapsamındaki
suçlar hariç." deniyor ve bugün ülkemizde en fazla ihtiyaç duyulan şey, hakikaten, demokrasi, eşitlik, özgürlük ve tabii ki adalet, ilk başta da adalet geliyor ve bu kanunda maalesef, bunların hiçbiri söz konusu değil. Şunu da söyleyeyim: Demin, böyle kendimce bir şema çıkardım. Şimdi, ayrımcılığı ifade etmek için sadece birkaç madde söyleyeceğim. Yargı herkes için yargılama yapar, tabii hâkim ilkesine göre önceden bellidir değil mi kimin nerede yargılanacağı? Ayşe ve Baran, ikisi de yargılanıyor; TMK kapsamındaki suçlardan Baran yargılanıyor, diğeri Kayseri'de oturan Ayşe olsun. Ne oluyor? Ayşe'ye ceza veriliyor, cezasını yatıyor, denetimli serbestlikten yararlanıyor, İnfaz Yasası'ndan yararlanıyor, asla ikametgâhının olduğu ilin dışında bir cezaevine gönderilmiyor, infazı uzatılmıyor, hücrede kalmıyor, kötü muameleye maruz kalmıyor yani bu İnfaz Yasası'nı tarif ediyorum. Peki, Baran'a ne oluyor? Baran ceza alıyor, TMK kapsamında bu artırılıyor, yarı oranında artırılıyor, infazı Ayşe'ye göre neredeyse 2 katına yakın, dörtte 3 oranında infazını yatıyor. Baran Diyarbakırlı olsun, Diyarbakır'da cezaevinde kalamıyor; Edirne'ye götürülüyor, Silivri'ye götürülüyor, Trabzon'a götürülüyor, başka bir ile, binlerce kilometre uzakta bir cezaevine götürülüyor. Bu da yetmiyor, Baran cezasını tamamlıyor, kanuna göre şartla salıverilmesi lazım; hayır, burada yine çıkarılan bir yasaya göre gözlem kurulu rapor veriyor, diyor ki: "Sen elektriği fazla harcadın, ben senin infazını uzatıyorum üç ay." Başka bir üç ay daha geçiyor, tekrar çağırıyorlar Baran'ı -bunlar bildiğim örnekler bu arada- gözlem kurulu çağırıyor -bunları gerçekten espri olsun diye söylemiyorum- "Sen fazla kitap okuyorsun, altı ay daha uzattım." diyor ve bunu uzattıkça uzatıyorlar, üç yıla kadar uzatılanlar var galiba. İnfaz süreleri uzatılıyor, hücrede tutuluyor bazıları. Mesela, geçen hafta, on gün önce Dumlu Cezaevine gittim -benim vekili olduğum il Erzurum- ağırlaştırılmış müebbet hapis cezası alanlara göre yapısı var, mimari yapısı ama görüştüğüm hiç kimse ağırlaştırılmış müebbet değildi, tutukluydu; hepsi tek kişilik hücrelerde kalıyordu. "Niye?" dedim, Müdürle görüştüm, idareyle "Bu, bizim sorunumuz değil. Adalet Bakanlığı buraya sevk etmiş. Bu nedenle biz burada yatırıyoruz." dediler. Telefonla görüşme süresinden açık görüş süresine kadar, ailelerin aranmasına kadar tamamen bir ayrımcılık var. Eşit değil, Baran ve Ayşe kanun önünde, Anayasa önünde eşit değil çünkü onun kimliği, dili, varlığı reddediliyor çünkü kabul edilmiyor; bunun kökeni budur, bir ret ve inkâr politikasıdır yani bunun istediğimiz kadar aksini söyleyelim. Denetimli serbestlikten de yararlanamıyor. Şimdi, önümüzdeki infaz taslağına göre evde infazdan da yararlanamıyor -konutta infaz diyoruz galiba- işte, indirim oranları var, ondan da, hiçbir şeyden yararlanamıyor Baran ya! İşte budur, adaletsizlik burada. Hani hukuk ve kanunlar geneldi, hani soyuttu, hani bütün yurttaşlara eşit uygulanırdı? Hayır, bu ülkede bir eşitlik hukuku yoktur ve tam da bu eşitlik hukuku için umudu büyüttüğümüz, bunun çalışmasını yaptığımız ve inandığımız bir barış ve kardeşlik sürecinin görüşüldüğü, tartışıldığı bir iklimde böyle bir kanun teklifinin gelmiş olması hakikaten büyük bir talihsizlik olmuştur. Yani, ben en yumuşak hâliyle söylüyorum. Yoksa bu arada, beklentiyi de biz yaratmadık. Biz her konuşmamızda "Pazarlık yok, şart yok." dedik. "Biz bu sürecin her hâlükârda yürümesinden yanayız." dedik. İşte buna ilişkin, maalesef, yine keyfiyete ve ayrımcılığa dayalı bir kanun teklifi
önümüzde duruyor ve suç türünden, suçlunun kişiliğinden ayrı uygulamalarla karşı karşıyayız infaz sürelerinde ve hakikaten bu şartlı salıvermeden bile faydalanmamalarının hukuki anlamda, Türkiye'nin taraf olduğu ulusalüstü sözleşmelere göre de hiçbir dayanağı yok. Yani kanun hiç gelmeseydi, emin olun, bu kadar büyük bir hayal kırıklığı, daha doğrusu bu kadar büyük bir infial oluşmazdı çünkü çalışmalar devam ediyor olacaktı. Önümüzdeki günlerde umarız ve dileriz ki İnfaz Yasası da Türkiye hukukunda bulunan bütün bu ayrımcı uygulamalar da ayıklanır, yargı kararlarından da umarım yargı da... Uygulamada nasıl oluyor? Uygulamada da maalesef, kişiye göre, kimliğe göre, dediğim gibi, kadın olmana göre kararlar değişebiliyor. Mesela "Kadına yönelik suçlarda cezasızlık politikası var." derken biz meselenin pratiğinden söz ediyoruz; biz "İşkence failleri ceza almıyor." derken kamu görevlilerinin korunmasından söz ediyoruz; biz Kürtlere yönelik uygulamalarda en ciddi suçlarda yaşam hakkında zaman aşımı ve beraat kararlarını anlattığımızda "Kürt'e karşı suç işlemek serbest." diyoruz. Yahu, Musa Anter'in davası kapatıldı ya! Bu ülkede "koca çınar" dediğimiz, bizim "Ape Musa" dediğimiz büyük bir gazeteciden söz ediyorum. Yani sabit olan bütün fiiller bu ülkede zaman aşımı ve beraatle sonuçlandırıldı ama artık bu yetti gerçekten! Hiçbirimiz bunları hak etmiyoruz, Türkiye'de 86 milyon yurttaş bunu hak etmiyor. Sadece Kürtler değil, her bir birey de bu haksızlıktan, bu ayrımcılıktan, bu eşitsizlikten nasibini alıyor çünkü ayrımcı uygulamalar bulaşıcıdır. Mesela, kayyum uygulaması. Üçüncü dönemdir bizim belediyelere kayyum atanıyor ve bu dönem maalesef CHP'li belediyelere yönelik de bu uygulama genişledi. Mesela, bunun hukukla izahı mümkün değildir, adaletle izahı mümkün değildir, gece yarıları, sabaha karşı insanların evlerine baskın yapılarak gözaltına alınmaları hukuki değildir; bunu kesinlikle kabul edilemez buluyoruz. Sonuçta, sabit ikametgâh sahibi olan, istenildiğinde mahkemeye, savcılığa gidebilecek olan, seçilmiş, halk oylarıyla seçilmiş temsilcilerin, belediye meclisi üyelerinin, belediye başkanlarının bu şekilde gözaltına alınmasını doğru bulmadığımızı da ifade etmek istiyorum ve bu, bizim de sıkça yaşadığımız bir mesele.
Son olarak şunu da söyleyeyim, Kürt meselesi ve ret ve inkâr meselesinden söz etmişken belediyelerle bağlantılı bölümünü söyleyeyim. Belki vekil arkadaşlar bilmiyordur, Türkiye'de çok yeni bir dava tipi var, biliyor musunuz? Kent uzlaşısı davaları, kent uzlaşısı. Bu ne demek? Kürtlere... Üstelik savcı bunu talep yazısına yazmış ya, inanamadım o savcıya, demiş ki: "Bu seçimler vasıtasıyla belediye meclisinde yer almak, belediye başkanını belirlemek." Yani oy vermek, oy almak. Kürt'ün İstanbul'da, Ankara'da, İzmir'de, Antalya'da, Kocaeli'de ve Türkiye'nin diğer bütün illerinde -batı illerinde- oy alıp vermesi suç konusu diye yargılama konusu şu anda. Ben on gün önce İstanbul'da kent uzlaşısı davasını izledim; hakikaten, hani "İzahı olmayanın mizahı olur." derler ya, mizah da yapamıyoruz çünkü arkadaşlarımız tutuklu, çünkü cezaevindeler. Sonuçta, partiler tabii ki görüşürler, tabii ki ittifak yaparlar, Türkiye Cumhuriyeti vatandaşı herkes tabii ki aday olabilir, tabii ki oy isteyebilir, tabii ki istediğine oy verebilir. Umarız, bu ucube durumun da sonlanması için gerekli bir çaba gösterilir.
Sayın Başkan, son olarak şunu söylüyorum: Umarım, bu eleştirilerimiz, bu değerlendirmelerimiz ve önerilerimiz bu eşitsizliğin, bu ayrımcılığın en azından Komisyon aşamasında giderimine katkısı olur; bir daha gözden geçirmememiz için hiçbir sebep yok. Ben bütün milletvekili arkadaşları da bu
değişikliğe onay vermeye davet ediyorum.
Teşekkür ediyorum.
records.englishTranslation:
MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Thank you, Mr. President.
I also greet everyone with respect.
Yes, another judicial package, we are in the Commission hall again and we are going over the previous amendments for the first time. Judicial packages, packages. I say this from a place that knows the last decade, there are also friends who know, and unfortunately, one of the main agendas of Türkiye right now has suddenly become the execution package. From where? Because an expectation has been created and before that, of course, the political climate we live in is very important. After many years
The demand for peace and democratic society has come into being, and in fact, there has been a broad consensus among everyone, whether they express it loudly or not, that this peace should be established, that the rule of law should be valid in Türkiye, and that democracy is as great a need as bread and water. As a friend of yours who is constantly in the field, I say that everywhere we go - of course the economy is important, living conditions are of course important, femicide is of course important - but the first question asked is "How will this process happen?" He continues, "We hope it will come to an end, we hope this conflictual environment will end, we hope this discrimination will end, and of course we hope this democracy will be fully realized." Now, it is important that in such a period of time, this execution package and judicial package come with such a discriminatory and unequal content; This is the real issue being discussed. Of course, in this process, there is no negotiation in Mr. Bahçeli's call, Mr. Öcalan's February 27 statement on this issue and all the statements made afterwards. That's right, we express this over and over again. There is no give-and-take process, no negotiation, but what is there? There is the establishment of peace and, of course, the construction of democracy, and there is the creation of a democratic society together. There are no conditions, no bargaining, but there are requirements. There is no need for it to be a requirement on some issues, that is, if there will be no weapons and if we are to wake up to a different climate, these discriminatory practices that currently exist in the law must also end; This is a necessity, I underline it. What did I first feel when looking at this execution package? Believe me, I saw the Kurdish issue first; Once again, there is no law for the Kurds, again there is no democracy. Well, let me underline this, not only to the Kurds, but also to those who stand by the Kurds, to those who are their friends, and to those who oppose them. I'll explain what I mean in a moment. I have been involved in many judicial package discussions. I will not explain the harmonization laws, I am saying this by referring to the advanced steps taken by the Justice and Development Party after 2002 under the name of "harmonization with the European Union". Then, in 2007, steps were taken again, changes were made in the PVSK, again the security issue took precedence over all freedoms after September 11, so unfortunately we all experienced this together in terms of recent history, and the TMK discussions never ended in Türkiye. Why does the Anti-Terror Law exist, why does it increase the penalties, why does freedom of thought and expression continue to be a crime? Why is every word, every activity, every democratic reaction associated - in quotation marks - with terrorism?

These discussions are not new, that is, they cover the last decades for us and unfortunately, freedom of expression has always continued to be a problem and Turkey is unfortunately a country that has faced numerous, countless violation decisions regarding freedom of expression before the European Court of Human Rights - I will not give numbers. As someone who made many applications to the European Court of Human Rights during the years I was a lawyer, I am partially familiar with those case files, that is, which articles are within the scope of violation. The effect of this TMK has now become such that an ordinary citizen is punished for membership in an organization or committing a crime on behalf of the organization for reasons such as participating in a social demonstration or making a statement on any issue, standing next to someone, being behind a visual, etc. They still do, so this is not old, these are ongoing practices. In other words, journalists can be arrested just for reporting news about the organization, and unfortunately, this continues. In short, the TMK violates all rights and fundamental rights regarding freedom of thought and expression, and this is still
continues. In fact, the Anti-Terrorism Law is contrary to the Constitution, but it was drafted as a law and with the amendments made, the TMK rose like a wall in front of rights and freedoms, and unfortunately, this wall continues.
I think we are here largely as lawyers. For example, none of the crimes regulated in Article 3713 are actually unpunished in Turkish Penal Code No. 5237, they all exist, that is, all crimes are defined. Again, special investigation procedures are included in Article 5271, the Code of Criminal Procedure, but for some reason, they are added to an additional stitch, colloquially called a double stitch, and the pressure is increased. Including the package we have today, excluding TMK. What does TMK mean? They say except the Kurds. Every speech, every word, every action, every political activity or politicking you make about the Kurds and the Kurdish issue is evaluated within the scope of the TMK. Unfortunately, today, those who committed the most heinous crimes, people who were actually excluded and unaccepted in society, were released during the pandemic period with the Covid law. At that time, we made a very strong opposition and said that you were ordering the death of people who were inside for political reasons. Well, we expressed all these things that we are talking about now, and unfortunately we were right. What's happening? Well, those who committed crimes such as harassment, rape, theft, gangs, drugs have benefited from Covid, but our friends from the Kobani case are still in prison and I think it has been a year and ten days, the time is still increasing, and a reasoned decision has still not been written. I'm just giving this as an example. This is the Kobani case, in which all our Central Executive Board members at that time and our previous co-chairmen, Dear Figen Yüksekdağ and Selahattin Demirtaş, are still in prison, in their 9th year, their nine years will be completed in five months. It's so easy to say nine years, right? They have been deprived of their freedom for nine years and have only one crime - in quotation marks - to engage in politics and to be in opposition.
Again, in all the laws enacted at that time, except for the TMK, except for the Kurds, except for the opposition, laws were enacted and regulated, and today, here is "Covid regulation should be applied equally." We said, there was really a great expectation in the statements made by the government spokesmen.

There are not only those who have been discriminated against, but also tens of thousands of people who are out just because of Covid and need to go to prison again, and there are also suspects within the scope of the TMK, which needs to be implemented. None of them were included in the package and right now there is a real state of outrage in prisons, our phones are not ringing, our message boxes are not really empty. In the relevant bill, for example, a reduction is foreseen for crimes such as looting, theft - let me repeat - intentional wounding, establishing an organization, etc., which are crimes at any time, anywhere and for everyone, but unfortunately, acts that do not contain violence and are completely within the scope of freedom of thought, expression or democratic reaction are excluded from the scope due to the case files. So, how else can we describe discrimination? I don't know that either. There is a clear state of inequality.
We all learned in law schools that laws can be general or abstract, when you read that law you don't know who it is for or who it is for, but in our country, laws can now be classified according to person, identity, beliefs, gender. I will also evaluate the practice mentioned by Mr. Feti. It is true, there is a problem in the implementation, but there is also discrimination in the law itself, in its essence. It does not need to go to the practitioner's hand. Now, we are making arrangements, we say: "Except for this, this, these items." There is no need to even say it anymore." Within the scope of TMK
Except for crimes." It is said and what is needed most in our country today is indeed democracy, equality, freedom and of course justice, justice comes first and unfortunately, none of these are in question in this law. Let me also say this: I just came up with a diagram of my own. Now, I will just say a few items to express discrimination. The judiciary judges everyone, of course, according to the principle of the judge, it is clear in advance who will be tried where, right? Ayşe and Baran are both on trial; Baran is on trial for crimes within the scope of the TMK, the other one is Ayşe, who lives in Kayseri. What's happening? Ayşe is sentenced, she serves her sentence, she benefits from probation, she benefits from the Execution Law, she is never sent to a prison outside the province of her residence, her execution is not extended, she does not stay in a cell, she is not subjected to ill-treatment, that is how I describe this Execution Law. So, what happens to Baran? Baran is sentenced, this is increased within the scope of the TMK, it is increased by half, his execution time is almost twice as much as Ayşe's, and his execution time is 3 quarters. Even though Baran is from Diyarbakır, he cannot stay in prison in Diyarbakır; He is taken to Edirne, he is taken to Silivri, he is taken to Trabzon, he is taken to another province, to a prison thousands of kilometers away. This is not enough, Baran completes his sentence, he must be released on condition according to the law; No, here again, according to a law passed, the observation board reports and says: "You spent too much electricity, I am prolonging your execution for three months." Another three months pass, they call Baran again - these are examples I know, by the way - the observation board calls - I'm not really saying these as a joke - "You read too many books, I extended it for another six months.

" he says and they extend it longer and longer, I think there are some who have been extended up to three years. Execution periods are being extended, some are being kept in solitary cells. For example, last week, ten days ago, I went to Dumlu Prison - Erzurum, the province where I am the deputy - it has a structure, an architectural structure, for those sentenced to aggravated life imprisonment, but no one I met was aggravated life imprisonment, they were all in solitary cells. I said, "Why?" I talked to the Director, and the administration said, "This is our problem." not. The Ministry of Justice referred him here. That's why we're laying him down here." They said. There is a complete discrimination, from phone call time to open visitation time to calling families. They are not equal, Baran and Ayşe are not equal before the law, before the Constitution, because their identity, language, existence are rejected because they are not accepted; this is the root of this, it is a policy of rejection and denial, so let's say the opposite as much as we want. He cannot benefit from probation either. Now, according to the execution draft before us, he cannot benefit from execution at home either - we call it execution at home. I guess - there are discount rates, and he can't benefit from anything! This is the injustice. You know, law and laws were general, they were abstract, they were applied equally to all citizens? No, there is no law of equality in this country, and it is truly a great misfortune that such a law proposal has come in a climate where a peace and brotherhood process is discussed and discussed, where we have raised hopes for this law of equality. By the way, we did not create the expectation. We said, "There is no negotiation, there are no conditions." We said, "We are in favor of this process in any case." Unfortunately, this is a bill based on arbitrariness and discrimination.
We are faced with practices that are different from the type of crime and the personality of the criminal, in terms of execution periods, and in fact, not even benefiting from this conditional release has no basis in the legal sense, nor according to the supranational agreements to which Türkiye is a party. So, if the law had never been introduced, rest assured, there would not have been such a huge disappointment, or rather such a huge outrage, because the work would have been continuing. We hope and pray that in the coming days, the Execution Law and all these discriminatory practices in Turkish law will be eliminated, and I hope the judiciary will also follow the judicial decisions. How does it work in practice? Unfortunately, in practice, decisions may vary depending on the person, identity, and as I said, whether you are a woman. For example, "There is a policy of impunity for crimes against women." We are talking about the practice of the matter; We say "Perpetrators of torture are not punished." We are talking about the protection of public officials; When we talk about the statute of limitations and acquittals for the most serious crimes against Kurds, we say, "It is free to commit crimes against Kurds." we say. Wow, Musa Anter's case was closed! I am talking about a great journalist whom we call "Ape Musa", whom we call "big plane tree" in this country. In other words, all the established acts were concluded with statute of limitations and acquittal in this country, but this is really enough!

None of us deserve this, 86 million citizens in Türkiye do not deserve this. Not only the Kurds, but also every individual gets their share of this injustice, discrimination and inequality because discriminatory practices are contagious. For example, trustee application. Trustees have been appointed to our municipalities for the third term, and unfortunately, this practice has also expanded to CHP municipalities. For example, it is not possible to explain this with law, it is not possible to explain it with justice, it is not legal to raid people's homes and detain them in the middle of the night or in the morning; We find this completely unacceptable. Ultimately, I would like to express that we do not find it right to detain elected representatives, municipal council members and mayors in this way, who have a fixed residence and can go to the court or prosecutor's office when requested, and this is an issue we frequently experience.
Finally, let me say this, while we are talking about the Kurdish issue and the issue of rejection and denial, let me mention the part related to municipalities. Maybe your attorney friends don't know, there is a very new type of case in Türkiye, do you know? Urban reconciliation cases, urban reconciliation. What does this mean? To the Kurds. Moreover, the prosecutor wrote this in the request letter, I couldn't believe that prosecutor, he said: "To take part in the municipal council and determine the mayor through these elections." In other words, voting is getting votes. Kurdish voting in Istanbul, Ankara, Izmir, Antalya, Kocaeli and all other provinces of Türkiye - the western provinces - is currently under trial because it is a crime. I watched the urban reconciliation case in Istanbul ten days ago; Indeed, you know, "Those who have no explanation have humor." As they say, we cannot make humor because our friends are detained, because they are in prison. After all, of course the parties meet, of course they make alliances, of course anyone who is a citizen of the Republic of Turkey can be a candidate, of course they can ask for votes, of course they can vote for whomever they want. We hope that necessary efforts will be made to put an end to this freak situation.
Mr. President, finally I say this: I hope that our criticisms, evaluations and suggestions will contribute to the elimination of this inequality and discrimination, at least at the Commission stage; There's no reason why we shouldn't reconsider. This is what all my fellow MPs are saying.
I invite you to approve the change.
Thank you.
