Yes, this objection is not just an economic objection; this is also
It is an objection to how foreign policy is established. First of all, I would like to state that this issue is not just a matter of financial choices, it is a regime issue, a society issue, a matter of democracy and freedoms. Therefore, we oppose the economy of war to the economy of peace. Peace economy is the transfer of resources from war devices to social production, and the budget should plan the real needs of the people, not just with the security discourse. Real security is not military capacity but the life assurance of the people. NATO increased its defense expenditures to 5 percent of the gross national product, the global arms race grew, but Türkiye, our country, approved this without any objection and without any social debate. This has not gone unnoticed, and of course we see that it continues to grow. In fact, peaceful diplomacy is the only way for Türkiye to build both its regional and global reputation. Collaboration, not hostility, and cooperation, not threats, should be the basis.
And another topic is the women's issue. In fact, it is an issue that is constantly overlooked and left out of discussion. We women constitute half of the society and we attach great importance to the gender budget issue. Undoubtedly, the basis of social peace is women's freedom. Therefore, for us, foreign policy is also the expression of the gender regime. According to the World Economic Forum 2025 Report, unfortunately, Türkiye ranks 135th among 148 countries in the gender gap category.

Again, Türkiye is the clear champion in the OECD's employment and women's employment category, and unfortunately, it does not give up its championship.
Türkiye's diplomatic tradition has been shaped by a male-dominated mentality and the steps taken have been taken with masculine codes, and unfortunately, the picture we see is completely male. There are very few exceptions, we women, our foreign policy is entrusted to men; We do not accept this, we reject it, I say that at least half of it should be women, and half of it should have women's labor and visibility.
İSMAİL GÜNEŞ (Uşak) - Most here.
MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - We object.
İSMAİL GÜNEŞ (Uşak) - Maximum.
MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Do not object to everything.
CHAIRMAN MEHMET MUŞ - Dear friends, more may be requested. Please.
MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Unfortunately, the presence of women in the field of diplomacy has often remained at a symbolic level, and this is a matter of handling the issue, the issue of women not being at the center of the solution, and of course the issue of peace processes being disconnected from the axis of justice. As women, we defend our equal participation in diplomacy not only as a matter of representation but as a principle of political re-existence.
Now, our Deputy Berdan said this: It is no secret that the unresolved Kurdish issue is the issue that Türkiye faces most in the international arena, but unfortunately, in such a period, during the solution and peace period, the right to cross-border operations was extended for another three years. Yes, the motion was accepted and this is also the will to continue security-oriented policies.
Dear Minister, I watched you hug Shara; I honestly don't know what to say. I'm not saying don't hug, you hugged yesterday's criminal and today's most respected person, for whom a 10 million dollar bounty was placed, I have no objection, but I wish you would give the same hug to the representatives of the people whom we truly call brothers. Why don't you hug Mazlum Abdi? Why don't you get together with İlham Ahmed? If you are a friend of the Kurds, 5 million Kurds live there with different identities and your perspective of friendship, your perspective of solution will bring peace and tranquility there, and be sure that it will contribute greatly to Türkiye. I listened to one of your speeches, you said in a television program that the SDF was supported under the pretext of fighting against ISIS. What excuse, Mr. Minister? Tens of thousands of people fell to the ground there while fighting against ISIS. This is not an excuse; This is a reality of life and accept it now.
And there is another thing that interests me: the validity of the March 10 agreement.
matter of implementation. You often say, and we say it too, that, as DEM PARTY, we advocate for democracy to prevail in Syria, Northeast Syria and the whole of Syria, and we advocate for a democratic Syrian Republic. You are talking about the implementation of this agreement, but on March 13, Shara declared an interim constitution, and what a coincidence you met with Shara on March 13, what a coincidence, you were there again when Shara met with Trump. Of course, we know very well that it is not a coincidence, and we have never heard this from your mouth or from the President's mouth: "A new democratic constitution for Syria. A Syria where everyone will live equally and freely." Why don't you express your defense?

We want to hear this because this will be the real solution there, Türkiye should encourage this with its policies.
Again, the issue of accession to the European Union is no longer even off the ground for discussion. Türkiye, one of the founding members of the Council of Europe, is experiencing one of the most severe processes and has initiated an infringement procedure in front of the Committee of Ministers of the Council of Europe in 2022. This is a heavy sanctions process and we all know that the EU Türkiye report notes that there is a regression in terms of democracy, rule of law and fundamental rights.
Why don't we hear a sentence from Mr. Hakan Fidan for the implementation of the decisions of Selahattin Demirtaş, Figen Yüksekdağ, Osman Kavala? I'm sure you encounter it every day abroad, if not in every conversation, you encounter it frequently. And the Minister of Justice boasted: "We comply 91 percent." he says. Then, do we all have the right to disobey 10 percent of the laws and the Constitution? Could such a statement be possible? But I would like to emphasize here that the greatest responsibility in this matter also lies with you. In particular, the decisions of the European Court of Human Rights. These days, millions are waiting for the release of their beloved Yüksekdağ and Demirtaş. You make a lot of statements on this subject, devote one of your statements to this. As Türkiye's Minister of Foreign Affairs, we want to hear from you the call for its implementation.
And - I think my time has run out - I would like to say this lastly: Lawlessness is the biggest dead end. We always invite Türkiye to the law, and without law there can be neither peace nor democracy nor security nor the future of the people can be secured.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - I'm wrapping it up.
MAYOR MEHMET MUŞ - Come on, wait a minute.
MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - The summary of what I said is this, Mr. Minister: Our advocacy is more than a foreign policy program, it is a peace economy; this is a democratization project; This is an issue of women's liberation; This is also the democratic solution program of the Kurdish problem. We say that an economy that does not produce justice cannot produce peace. We say that diplomacy without women cannot produce social peace. We want a regime in which the Kurds are not equal to each other and cannot produce regional stability. On the other hand, we believe that it is possible to overcome this blockage created by war economy, male-dominated diplomacy and security-centered foreign policy, because the common will of the people increases the power of peace, not militarism. We want to build the future of this country on freedom, not fear. Don't be afraid of peace, be afraid of war, Mr. Minister.
Thank you.

records.originalTurkish:
MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Uzatıyor musunuz, önce peşin söyleyeyim.
OTURUM BAŞKANI NİLGÜN ÖK - Şöyle: Beş dakikayı uzatmıyoruz. Bugün gerçekten rekor kırıyoruz, Komisyon üyesi olmayan 45 milletvekilimiz sırada bekliyor; önceden de var, tamamladığımızda totalde 73 milletvekilimiz konuşmuş olacak.
MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Bir dakikayı yediniz.
OTURUM BAŞKANI NİLGÜN ÖK - Bir dakika da ben yedim, evet. Neyse ben on dakika yerine dokuz dakika konuşmuştum, ona sayayım.
MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Teşekkürler.
Sayın Bakan, değerli milletvekilleri; ben hepinizi saygıyla selamlıyorum.
Bu kadar kısa sürede size bir ilden ve yapılamayan 2 tünelden söz edeceğim. Ben Erzurum Milletvekiliyim. Ulaşım, hepimizin bildiği üzere, kamusal bir hak ve aynı zamanda dünyada da medeniyetin ve gelişimin önemli göstergelerinden bir tanesi. 2 tünel dedim, evet, Palandöken Dağı'nın âdeta güney ilçeleriyle arasına bir set çektiğini ifade etmek isterim. Güney ilçeleri ve kuzey ilçeleri, Erzurum'da çok bilinen bir tabirdir çünkü güney ilçelerinde ağırlıklı olarak Kürt yurttaşlar yaşar, kuzey ilçelerinde Kürtler daha az bulunur ve bu ayrımcılık aslında bütün verilere yansıyor tıpkı yollara yansıdığı gibi.
Sayın Bakan, dinlerseniz mutlu olurum.
Sosyoekonomik gelişmişlik araştırmasına göre de sıralamayı sadece söyleyeceğim, ilçe gelişmişlik sıralaması gruplarından Karaçoban 952, Karayazı 966, Tekman 969; Erzurum'un diğer ilçeleriyle de arasında uçurum olduğunu ifade etmek isterim. Ben Erzurum'dan Karadeniz Bölgesi'ne de gittim, bütün ilçelerini gezdim ve hakikaten kuzey ile güney Paris ile bir köyü karşılaştırmak gibi neredeyse, abartmıyorum; bizzat o yolları, köyleri gezen bir milletvekili olarak söylüyorum. Diyeceksiniz ki: "O, büyükşehrin alanı, köy yolları il özel idaresinin." Orayı size sormayacağım, onun muhatabı ayrı ama iktidarın bir Bakanı olarak size bugün Çirişli ve Tekman Tüneli'ni soracağım. Her iki tüneli de... Daha doğrusu, Tekman'da henüz başlayan bir şey yok da öylesine bir şey var yani ilerleyen bir şey yok.
Çirişli Tüneli'ne 2 defa gittim hatta video da çektim, gittiğimde yapımı durmuştu, şu anda bildiğim kadarıyla devam ediyor. 2017 yılında başlamış, Bingöl ve Erzurum arasında bir tünel ve bu tünel Erzurum'u yani bir anlamda, aslında, Karadeniz'i de Güneydoğu'ya, Akdeniz'e bağlayacak çok önemli bir yol. Neden 2017 yılından beri bir ilerleme sağlanmadı diye sormak isterim. Bu ayırımcılık burada da devam ettiriliyor, bu bilinçli mi?
Tekman Tüneli'ne ilişkin Binali Yıldırım'ın döneminde Cumhurbaşkanının talimat verdiğini de ifade etmek isterim. Tekman'a geçtim. 70 yılında ilk girişimler başlamış ve 2025 yılındayız. Her seçim döneminde... Geçen seçimde de bizzat Cumhurbaşkanı yapmış olduğu mitingde Tekman Tüneli'ne atıfta bulundu ve bu tünelin bitmesi gerektiğini ifade etti. 70 yılında girişimlerin başladığı,
Binali Bey'e talimat verildiği tarihten bugüne, 2025 yılına hâlâ ben kendim bizzat Erzurum-Tekman arasında kış şartlarında gittiğimde her an ölüm riski yaşıyorum ve orada yaşayan yurttaşlar her gün o riski yaşıyor ve kış şartlarının ne kadar çetin olduğunu sanırım size anlatmama gerek yok. Hakikaten Erzurum'da kışın yolculuk yapmak çok büyük bir risk almak demek, ben kendim de maalesef bizzat orada kaza yaptım ve o kazanın sonuçlarını hâlâ yaşıyorum bedenimde. Bu konudaki tutumumuzu merak ediyorum.
Kitapçığınıza hızla baktım, şöyle bir tespitiniz var: Son yirmi üç yılda tünellerde yüzde 1.566 artış olduğu ifade ediliyor yanlış bakmadıysam, diğer ayrıntılara girmeyeceğim. Yani hakikaten son yirmi üç yılı Taş Devri'yle mi kıyaslayalım Sayın Bakan? Son elli yılda da arttı, artık dünya uzaya gidiyor. Yani son "Yirmi üç yılda bunu yaptık." demektense somut olarak size bir ilin 2 tünelini soruyorum ve hakikaten cevabını bütün Erzurumlular gibi... Onlar adına soruyorum: Neden yapılmıyor? Karadeniz'e giden yollarda her adım başında tüneller varken niye Tekman Tüneli yapılmıyor? Üstelik, 12,5 kilometrelik bir tünel, yolu 35 kilometre azaltacak ve insanların can güvenliğini sağlayacak kamusal bir haktan söz ediyoruz. Bu ayrımcılığı ortadan kaldıralım; kuzey ve doğuyu, batı ile güneyi artık ayırmayalım, yurttaşları ayırmayalım. Talebimizi size bizzat iletmek istiyorum ve bu 2 tünel hakkında en kısa zamanda bir icraat görmek istediğimizi de ifade etmek istiyorum.
Teşekkür ediyorum.
records.englishTranslation:
MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Are you making it longer? Let me tell you in advance.
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION NİLGÜN ÖK - As follows: We do not extend the five minutes. We are really breaking a record today, 45 of our deputies who are not members of the Commission are waiting in line; It has already existed, when we complete it, a total of 73 deputies will have spoken.
MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - You just took a minute.
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION NİLGÜN ÖK - Wait a minute, I ate it, yes. Anyway, I talked for nine minutes instead of ten, so I'll count it as ten.
MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Thank you.
Dear Minister, dear MPs; I greet you all with respect.
In such a short time, I will tell you about a province and 2 tunnels that could not be built. I am Erzurum Deputy. As we all know, transportation is a public right and also one of the important indicators of civilization and development in the world. I said 2 tunnels, yes, I would like to express that Palandöken Mountain has literally created a barrier between itself and the southern districts. Southern districts and northern districts are a well-known expression in Erzurum because predominantly Kurdish citizens live in the southern districts, there are fewer Kurds in the northern districts, and this discrimination is actually reflected in all the data, just as it is reflected in the roads.
Dear Minister, I would be happy if you would listen.
I will just say the ranking according to the socioeconomic development research. Among the district development ranking groups, Karaçoban 952, Karayazı 966, Tekman 969; I would like to express that there is a gap between Erzurum and other districts. I went from Erzurum to the Black Sea Region, visited all its districts, and it is almost like comparing a village with northern and southern Paris, I am not exaggerating; I say this as a member of parliament who personally travels those roads and villages. You will say: "It is the area of ​​​​the metropolitan city, the village roads are of the special provincial administration." I will not ask you about that, his interlocutor is different, but as a Minister of the government, I will ask you about Çirişli and Tekman Tunnel today. Both tunnels. More precisely, there is nothing that has started yet in Tekman, but nothing that is progressing.
I went to Çirişli Tunnel twice and even took a video. When I went there, its construction had stopped, but as far as I know, it continues. It is a tunnel between Bingöl and Erzurum, which started in 2017, and this tunnel is a very important road that will connect Erzurum, in a sense, the Black Sea, to the Southeast and the Mediterranean. I would like to ask why there has been no progress since 2017. This discrimination continues here too, is this conscious?
I would also like to state that the President gave instructions regarding the Tekman Tunnel during Binali Yıldırım's term. I switched to Tekman. The first initiatives started in 70 and we are in 2025. Every election season. In the last election, the President himself referred to the Tekman Tunnel in his rally and stated that this tunnel should be finished. Initiatives started in the 70's,
From the date Binali Bey was given the instruction to today, until 2025, when I travel between Erzurum and Tekman in winter conditions, I am still at risk of death at any moment, and the citizens living there live that risk every day, and I think I do not need to tell you how harsh the winter conditions are.

Indeed, traveling in Erzurum in winter means taking a huge risk. Unfortunately, I had an accident there myself and I still experience the consequences of that accident in my body. I wonder about our stance on this issue.
I took a quick look at your booklet, you have the following observation: It is stated that there has been a 1,566 percent increase in tunnels in the last twenty-three years. Unless I read correctly, I will not go into other details. So, should we really compare the last twenty-three years with the Stone Age, Mr. Minister? It has increased in the last fifty years, the world is now going to space. So the last "We did this in twenty-three years." Rather than saying, I ask you concretely about the 2 tunnels of a province and the answer is really like all the people of Erzurum. I ask on their behalf: Why isn't it done? Why isn't the Tekman Tunnel built when there are tunnels at every step on the roads leading to the Black Sea? Moreover, we are talking about a 12.5 kilometer tunnel, a public right that will reduce the road by 35 kilometers and ensure people's life safety. Let's eliminate this discrimination; Let us no longer separate north and east, west and south, let us not separate citizens. I would like to convey our request to you personally and express that we want to see action regarding these 2 tunnels as soon as possible.
Thank you.

I will just say the ranking according to the socioeconomic development research. Among the district development ranking groups, Karaçoban 952, Karayazı 966, Tekman 969; I would like to express that there is a gap between Erzurum and other districts. I went from Erzurum to the Black Sea Region, visited all its districts, and it is almost like comparing a village with northern and southern Paris, I am not exaggerating; I say this as a member of parliament who personally travels those roads and villages. You will say: "It is the area of ​​​​the metropolitan city, the village roads are of the special provincial administration." I will not ask you about that, his interlocutor is different, but as a Minister of the government, I will ask you about Çirişli and Tekman Tunnel today. Both tunnels. More precisely, there is nothing that has started yet in Tekman, but nothing that is progressing.
I went to Çirişli Tunnel twice and even took a video. When I went there, its construction had stopped, but as far as I know, it continues. It is a tunnel between Bingöl and Erzurum, which started in 2017, and this tunnel is a very important road that will connect Erzurum, in a sense, the Black Sea, to the Southeast and the Mediterranean. I would like to ask why there has been no progress since 2017. This discrimination continues here too, is this conscious?
I would also like to state that the President gave instructions regarding the Tekman Tunnel during Binali Yıldırım's term. I switched to Tekman. The first initiatives started in 70 and we are in 2025. Every election season. In the last election, the President himself referred to the Tekman Tunnel in his rally and stated that this tunnel should be finished. Initiatives started in the 70's,
From the date Binali Bey was given the instruction to today, until 2025, when I travel between Erzurum and Tekman in winter conditions, I am still at risk of death at any moment, and the citizens living there live that risk every day, and I think I do not need to tell you how harsh the winter conditions are.

Indeed, traveling in Erzurum in winter means taking a huge risk. Unfortunately, I had an accident there myself and I still experience the consequences of that accident in my body. I wonder about our stance on this issue.
I took a quick look at your booklet, you have the following observation: It is stated that there has been a 1,566 percent increase in tunnels in the last twenty-three years. Unless I read correctly, I will not go into other details. So, should we really compare the last twenty-three years with the Stone Age, Mr. Minister? It has increased in the last fifty years, the world is now going to space. So the last "We did this in twenty-three years." Rather than saying, I ask you concretely about the 2 tunnels of a province and the answer is really like all the people of Erzurum. I ask on their behalf: Why isn't it done? Why isn't the Tekman Tunnel built when there are tunnels at every step on the roads leading to the Black Sea? Moreover, we are talking about a 12.5 kilometer tunnel, a public right that will reduce the road by 35 kilometers and ensure people's life safety. Let's eliminate this discrimination; Let us no longer separate north and east, west and south, let us not separate citizens. I would like to convey our request to you personally and express that we want to see action regarding these 2 tunnels as soon as possible.
Thank you.

records.section: Anayasa ve Adalet Komisyonları Üyelerinden Kurulu Karma Komisyon
records.title: Hâkimler ve Savcılar Kurulu üyeliği adayları seçimi

fields.Subject: Hâkimler ve Savcılar Kurulu üyeliği adayları seçimi

records.originalTurkish:
MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Teşekkürler Sayın Başkan.
Burada sevgili arkadaşımız, Meclis Başkan Vekilimiz, dostumuz Sırrı Süreyya Önder'e dair dilekleriniz, başsağlığı dilekleriniz için herkese ayrı ayrı teşekkür ediyoruz. Hakikaten çok büyük bir acı, çok büyük bir üzüntü. Toplumsal barış açısından aslında toplum bize çok şey söyledi. Yani hem hastanede on sekiz gün boyunca hem de taziyede, cenaze töreninde bence toplumsal barış konusunda çok önemli bir mesafe alındı. Sırrı Başkan giderken de aslında Türkiye'ye çok büyük bir borç bıraktı, hepimize bence. Siyaset erkine, bize de görev verdi. Bence Türkiye Büyük Millet Meclisinin, Türkiye'nin barışını, kardeşliğini, eşitliğini sağlamak konusunda siyaset erkinin de üstüne alınması gereken bir boyutu var; bunu paylaşmak istedim. Mekânı cennet olsun.
Sayın Başkan, çok kısa şunu söyleyeceğim: Ben 2017 Anayasa Komisyonu tartışmalarını dün gibi hatırlıyorum, ayrıntılara girmeyeceğim. Bu madde tartışılırken sabahlara kadar çalıştık, haftalarca çalıştık ve burada en önemli amaç şuydu: Hakikaten Mecliste HSK gibi bir kurulun seçiminde siyasi parti ayrımı gözetmeksizin mutabakata zorlamak yani amaç buydu. İktidar grubu da bu teklifi sunarken bunu uzun uzun aktarmıştı, hatta kuraya giderken de yani başka bir seçenek olmasın diye bu söylenmişti.
Benden önceki arkadaşlar, Mithat Hoca da ayrıntılı ifade etti, diğer arkadaşlar da. Bu yönüyle, bence, kuraya giderken de orada oylama şeklinin Anayasa 159'a göre yani bize göre subjektif yoruma hiçbir şekilde alan açmayacak şekilde her bir üye için ayrı ayrı gizli oyla yapılacağı yönünde lafzi bir durum var. Doğru, geçmiş seçimlerde bu kabul edilmedi, biz iki seçimde de bulunmadık, bunu doğru bulmadığımızı ifade ettik. Mutabakatın olması, ortaklaşmanın azami düzeyde sağlanması gerektiği noktasında çok konuşmamız oldu ama, hepimiz matematiği de aşağı yukarı biliriz siyaset kadar olmasa da yani şu çok açık: Bu, yani sizin ifade ettiğiniz, AK PARTİ Grubunun ifade ettiği şekilde, toplu oylama olması hâlinde, tek tek yapılmaması hâlinde zaten olur ya, hani muhalefetin oy vereceği isimler olursa, onların zaten kuraya girme şansı olmuyor. Yani işin adını koyalım, hiç böyle dolayısıyla anlatmanın anlamı yok. Bu nedenle, ona bile şans verilemiyor bu durumda, verilmiyor. Yani biz de buraya gelmeden önce arkadaşlarla bir toplantı yaptık, bu bizim de önerimizdi zaten; bunun değerlendirilmesi lazım. HSK'nin yapısı, aldığı kararlar, yargı üzerindeki durumu, bağlayıcılığı, bütün bunları çok konuştuk yani bu Meclis çatısı altında herhâlde söylemediğimiz söz kalmadı, bu gök kubbe altında da kalmadı. Yargı tarafsız ve bağımsız olmalı ve bu HSK yapısıyla değil, bunu hiç tartışmaya gerek yok ve bağımsız ve tarafsız olabilmesi için de aslında bence herkese, en çok da iktidar grubuna görev düşüyor ama şu kesin: Yani Anayasa’nın lafzı -yazım aşamasında olan bir vekil olarak söylüyorum- kesinlikle bu değildi yani oylamanın bu şekilde yapılacağı şeklinde bir tartışma yoktu, Bülent Bey de burada, Mithat Hoca da burada fakat sonradan matematiksel hesaplar, farklı tartışmalar, işte, adaylıklar; işte, istenilen isimler çıksın diye böyle bir oylama yöntemi benimsendi. Yani bu çok isabetsiz bir durum yaratır ve daha önceki kararları değiştirmeyelim diye yanlışta ısrar etmenin anlamı yoktur. Nereden dönersek oradan kârlı çıkarız diyorum.
Tekrar teşekkür ediyorum.
records.englishTranslation:
MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Thank you, Mr. President.
Here, we would like to thank everyone individually for your wishes and condolences regarding our dear friend, our Deputy Speaker of the Parliament, our friend Sırrı Süreyya Önder. It is truly a great pain, a great sadness. Society actually told us a lot in terms of social peace. So, in my opinion, a very important progress was made in terms of social peace, both during the eighteen days in the hospital and during the condolence and funeral ceremony. When Sırrı President left, he actually left a huge debt to Türkiye, I think to all of us. He gave a duty to the political power and to us. In my opinion, the Grand National Assembly of Türkiye has a dimension that the political power should also take on when it comes to ensuring Türkiye's peace, brotherhood and equality; I wanted to share this. May he rest in peace.
Mr. President, I will say this very briefly: I remember the 2017 Constitutional Commission discussions like it was yesterday, I will not go into details. While this article was being discussed, we worked until the morning, we worked for weeks, and the most important goal here was this: In fact, this was the goal, to force consensus in the Parliament, regardless of political party, in the election of a board like the HSK. The ruling group also explained this at length when presenting this proposal, and even during the draw, this was said so that there would be no other option.
Friends before me, Mithat Hodja also explained it in detail, and other friends as well. In this respect, in my opinion, there is a literal situation that when going to the draw, the voting will be done in accordance with Constitution 159, that is, by secret ballot for each member separately, which in our opinion will not leave any room for subjective interpretation. True, this was not accepted in the past elections, we did not participate in either election, we stated that we did not find this right. We have talked a lot about the need for consensus and cooperation at the maximum level, but we all know mathematics more or less, although not as much as politics, so this is very clear: This, as you expressed and the AK PARTY Group expressed, will happen in case of mass voting, if it is not done individually, you know, if the opposition has names to vote for, they do not have a chance to enter the draw anyway. So let's name the thing, it's just like that, so there's no point in explaining it. For this reason, even he cannot be given a chance in this case. So, we had a meeting with our friends before coming here, this was our suggestion anyway; This needs to be evaluated. We have talked a lot about the structure of the HSK, the decisions it makes, its status on the judiciary, its binding nature, all of these, so there is probably not a word left that we have not said under the roof of this Parliament, not under this sky. The judiciary must be impartial and independent, and not with this HSK structure, there is no need to discuss this, and in fact, I think everyone has a responsibility to be independent and impartial, especially the ruling group, but this is certain: I mean, the wording of the Constitution - I say this as a representative who is in the writing phase - was definitely not this, that is, there was no discussion that the voting would be done this way, Mr. Bülent is here, Mithat Hoca is also here, but later mathematical calculations, different discussions, here are the nominations; Well, such a voting method was adopted to ensure that the desired names were revealed.

In other words, this creates a very inaccurate situation and there is no point in insisting on the mistake so as not to change previous decisions. I say we will profit from wherever we turn.
Thank you again.
